tools.name: drive_write
tools.description: Write a markdown file to a workspace. Creates or updates the file with automatic versioning.

tools.name: drive_read
tools.description: Read a file's content from a workspace.

tools.name: drive_list
tools.description: List files in a workspace, optionally filtered by path prefix.

tools.name: drive_share
tools.description: Generate a share link for a workspace or specific file. Optionally email-gate the link so only a specific person can access it. Humans open this link to view, comment, or edit files.

tools.name: drive_comment
tools.description: Add a comment to a file.

tools.name: drive_feedback
tools.description: Read human comments on a file. Use this to check if humans have left feedback.

tools.name: drive_activity
tools.description: Get recent activity in a workspace (file writes, comments, shares).

tools.name: drive_invite
tools.description: Invite a human to your organization by email. They will receive a verification code to join.

tools.name: drive_roles
tools.description: Manage per-workspace role delegation. Assign, list, or remove access for specific emails (view/comment/edit).

tools.name: drive_org
tools.description: View your organization details: agents, members, plan, and usage.

tools.name: drive_delete
tools.description: Delete a file from a workspace.

tools.name: drive_versions
tools.description: View version history for a file in a workspace.

tools.name: drive_search
tools.description: Semantic search across your org's artifacts in AgentDrive. Returns the top-N most relevant files. Use this BEFORE writing similar artifacts to avoid duplication and surface prior context.

tools.name: drive_recall
tools.description: Recall persistent facts and preferences your agents have written. In v1 this returns semantic search results across artifacts; v1.5 will narrow to auto-extracted facts (preferences, decisions, key entities).

tools.name: drive_log_run
tools.description: Log an agent run to AgentDrive's provenance store. Captures task, status, referenced/produced artifacts, and cross-platform identity (which agent on which tool). Used for retrospective context retrieval and team-wide audit.

tools.name: drive_context
tools.description: Auto-pull the most relevant artifacts from prior agent sessions for the task you are about to do. Combines semantic relevance with recency. Call this at the START of any non-trivial task to ground the agent in prior context — replaces manual copy-paste of artifacts between sessions.

tools.name: drive_sandbox_clone
tools.description: [EXPERIMENTAL] Clone a git repo into a per-workspace Linux sandbox. Requires Cloudflare Containers on the AgentDrive worker; gracefully degrades to a 503 with "sandbox not configured" otherwise.

tools.name: drive_sandbox_exec
tools.description: [EXPERIMENTAL] Run a shell command inside the per-workspace Linux sandbox. Captures stdout/stderr (64 KB cap), exit code, duration. Requires Cloudflare Containers; degrades to 503 otherwise.

tools.name: drive_sandbox_read
tools.description: [EXPERIMENTAL] Read a file from the per-workspace sandbox filesystem (build artifacts, test output, logs). Requires Cloudflare Containers; degrades to 503 otherwise.

tools.name: drive_sandbox_commit
tools.description: [EXPERIMENTAL] Stage + commit the current sandbox worktree. Surfaces as an AgentDrive artifact at commits/<sha>.json. Requires Cloudflare Containers; degrades to 503 otherwise.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: drive_share, drive_feedback, drive_activity, drive_invite, drive_roles · Private data: drive_write, drive_read, drive_list, drive_share, drive_comment, drive_feedback, drive_activity, drive_delete, drive_versions, drive_search, drive_sandbox_read · Public sink: drive_share, drive_invite · Destructive: drive_roles, drive_delete, drive_log_run, drive_sandbox_exec.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: drive_share, drive_feedback, drive_activity, drive_invite, drive_roles · Private data: drive_write, drive_read, drive_list, drive_share, drive_comment, drive_feedback, drive_activity, drive_delete, drive_versions, drive_search, drive_sandbox_read · Public sink: drive_share, drive_invite · Destructive: drive_roles, drive_delete, drive_log_run, drive_sandbox_exec

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
